place: Cedar Glen West CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Cedar Glen West began as a 55+ cooperative retirement community in the early 1960s, featuring 826 one‑story attached homes.
The area that became Cedar Glen West was once part of the New Jersey Pine Barrens, where sandy soils supported timber and charcoal industries.
William Torrey, a real estate developer, named the township after Manchester, England, hoping it would grow into a rail and manufacturing hub.
Cedar Glen West’s cooperative structure provides residents with shared lawn care, snow removal, and trash collection, fostering a close‑knit community.
The CDP sits between Ridgeway Boulevard and Ridgeway Road, largely occupied by the private Cedar Glen West Inc. residential park.
